learnings.summary: claude: CLI "claude" rejected flags ["--effort","low","--model","haiku","-p","--no-input"]: error: unknown option '--no-input'.
learnings.detail: Failed args: ["--effort","low","--model","haiku","-p","--no-input"]

learnings.summary: claude: CLI "claude" rejected flags ["--model","haiku","-p","--no-input"]: error: unknown option '--no-input'. Update HEADLESS_F
learnings.detail: Failed args: ["--model","haiku","-p","--no-input"]

learnings.summary: When testing two branches side by side with worktrees, dispatch two parallel agents (one per branch) instead of running everything sequentially in one thread. Each agent can set up its worktree, install deps, start its dev server, and run tests independently.

When research or parallel work is needed, ALWAYS use the DevOps Pilot orchestrator (POST /api/orchestrator/spawn) to dispatch worker agents (Gemini, Codex, Grok, etc.) instead of using built-in subagent/Agent tools. The orchestrator is the correct dispatch mechanism; built-in agents bypass it and the user cannot see the work in the orchestrator UI.

Run-Query.ps1 in DevOps Pilot takes -File (path to a .ps1 file), not -Query (WIQL string). It is a script runner, not a WIQL executor. Write WIQL logic into a .ps1 in .ai-workspace first, then pass the path via -File.
learnings.detail: Error: A parameter cannot be found that matches parameter name Query. The script just does & $File after validating the file exists.

learnings.summary: Before calling an endpoint, verify it exists in the documented API reference. Guessed routes return 404 and waste a round-trip. When a field you want to filter on is not supported as a query parameter, pull what is available and filter the rest in memory.

When merging an array with existing data read from an API, always normalize the existing value first. If the source uses an unusual separator or raw string, naive concatenation can produce a single mashed-together entry instead of separate ones. Split on the expected delimiter with a regex that tolerates whitespace, then join back with a canonical separator.

WIQL does not allow ORDER BY on the Tags field. Queries with ORDER BY [System.Tags] fail with a 400 Bad Request. Order by Id, State, or WorkItemType instead. The error body is often empty, which makes this hard to diagnose.

sendTrace and sendException have different signatures. sendTrace is (message, severityLevel=1, properties={}) -- 3 args. sendException is (exceptionType, message, method, properties={}) -- 4 args. Do not use the 4-arg exception shape for sendTrace.

The xlsx (SheetJS) library strips all formatting (colors, borders, fonts, column widths, merged cells) when you convert a sheet to an array with sheet_to_json and rebuild with aoa_to_sheet. To preserve styles when modifying an Excel file, manipulate cells in-place on the existing worksheet object: use shiftRowsDown to move cells, then write new cell values directly with writeRow copying style from a reference row. Read with { cellStyles: true } and write with { cellStyles: true }.
learnings.detail: sheet_to_json -> modify array -> aoa_to_sheet destroys all Excel formatting. Instead: (1) read existing cell addresses, (2) shift rows down from insertion point, (3) write new values into individual cells, copying .s (style) from a nearby reference row. This preserves the original workbook formatting.

learnings.summary: The SheetJS (xlsx) npm package community edition does NOT preserve styles, colors, or formatting -- even with cellStyles:true. That is a Pro-only feature. Use exceljs instead, which reads and writes xlsx files with full style preservation (fills, fonts, borders, tab colors, merged cells, row heights, column widths). When modifying existing rows, unmerge any merged ranges first before clearing/rewriting cells, otherwise merged cell values bleed into new data.
learnings.detail: Previous learning about shiftRowsDown + cellStyles is wrong -- SheetJS community cannot do it. exceljs is the correct library. Key gotcha: if the original file has merged cells (e.g. A44:F44 for a TOTAL row), you must call ws.unMergeCells(range) BEFORE clearing and rewriting those rows, otherwise the merge persists and corrupts new data written to those cells.

learnings.summary: In markdown, a blank line inside a pipe table breaks the table. All table rows must be contiguous with no empty lines between them. When inserting new rows into an existing markdown table, never leave a blank line between the old last row and the new first row, or the new rows will render as raw pipe-delimited text instead of table cells.

When extracting Elementor sections from a page to use as templates, always verify the actual column count and structure rather than relying on design doc descriptions. The design notes may describe an idealized layout (e.g. 3-col [20%,60%,20%]) while the actual implementation uses a different structure (e.g. 1-col 100%). Always inspect the JSON data before coding against it.

Elementor section background_color set via API does not render on the frontend until the CSS cache is regenerated (open in Elementor editor + click Update). For immediate rendering without cache regeneration, use the CSS viewport breakout technique on the HTML widget itself: wrap content in an outer div with width:100vw; position:relative; left:50%; transform:translateX(-50%); background:#color, and cap inner content with max-width:1440px; margin:0 auto. Also clear _elementor_css meta to force cache regeneration on next load.

When using the CSS 100vw breakout technique (width:100vw; left:50%; transform:translateX(-50%)) inside Elementor HTML widgets, always add: (1) overflow-x:clip on the breakout div to prevent horizontal scrollbar from scrollbar-width mismatch, (2) max-width:100vw as fallback, (3) overflow:hidden on the parent Elementor section via custom_css, (4) zero out all padding on the Elementor column via custom_css (selector > .elementor-element-populated { padding: 0 !important; }). Without these, mobile views get horizontal overflow and inward padding gaps.

Elementor responsive widget settings (align_mobile, _margin_mobile, etc.) written via API do NOT take effect until the CSS cache is regenerated by opening the page in Elementor editor and clicking Update. For immediate mobile fixes without editor access, use custom_css on the section with @media queries and the selector keyword. Custom CSS is embedded inline and renders immediately. This is the only reliable way to fix mobile layout via API.

Push-AndPR.ps1 breaks when -Description contains commas, apostrophes, or other special characters. PowerShell string parsing fails with TerminatorExpectedAtEndOfString. Instead, push the branch manually with git push -u origin HEAD, then create the PR via curl to POST /api/pull-request with a JSON body, which handles special characters correctly.
Builder-style image audits can produce false positives when custom components render alt text from fields such as imageAltText or imageAltTag instead of alt or altText.
learnings.detail: Before bulk editing CMS image alt text, inspect the consuming component code and treat component-specific alt fields as valid. Do not rely only on a generic audit that recognizes alt and altText.

learnings.summary: System.Drawing Bitmap.Save may throw a generic GDI+ error when saving generated images into dot-prefixed workspace folders.
learnings.detail: If this happens, save the generated image to a normal writable path, verify the file exists, then clean it up after use. Do not assume the drawing code failed before testing a simpler output path.

learnings.summary: In PowerShell loops, Invoke-RestMethod failures can leave a previous response variable in place and make later logging look like a false success.
learnings.detail: Reset the response variable before each request or wrap Invoke-RestMethod in try/catch. On catch, log the failure separately and do not read stale response data from a previous iteration.

learnings.summary: curl.exe JSON POSTs from PowerShell can hang or time out when inline body quoting is misescaped.
learnings.detail: For local API writes from PowerShell, prefer Invoke-RestMethod with a hashtable converted by ConvertTo-Json, or write complex JSON to a temp file and post that. Avoid deeply escaped inline curl JSON bodies.

learnings.summary: Node fs.writeFile can hit EPERM when writing generated files into a dot-prefixed workspace folder.
learnings.detail: If Node cannot write a generated manifest or temporary output into a dot-prefixed workspace folder, emit the data to stdout or write to another verified writable path, then clean up. Do not keep retrying the same path.

learnings.summary: When parsing markdown table rows with node -e, always guard cells.length before indexing. Header separator rows (|---|---|) split into fewer cells than data rows and cause TypeError on cells[N].replace. Skip rows where cells.length is below the expected column count.
learnings.detail: Encountered TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading replace) while parsing a markdown table via l.split(|). The offending line was a style-laden STORY row that split unevenly due to embedded HTML. Fix: add `if (cells.length < EXPECTED) continue;` before destructuring, and strip HTML with replace(/<[^>]+>/g, ) before trimming.

learnings.summary: Builder.io plugin GET /entries/:model hangs/404s; use GET /content/:model?limit=N instead. /entries is not the real route.
learnings.detail: The instructions table lists Get-Entries.ps1 (-Model), but the REST route for listing entries is /api/plugins/builderio/content/MODEL_NAME?limit=N. Calling /api/plugins/builderio/entries/MODEL never returns.

learnings.summary: Builder.io /insights?issue=missing-alt under-reports: default detector only matches alt/altText siblings. Custom field names (imageAltTag, videoAltTag, desktopImageAltTag, featuredImage without alt sibling, etc.) are missed and counts come back as 0 even when hundreds of images lack alt.
learnings.detail: For accurate alt audits on spaces with custom component schemas, fetch /content/MODEL and walk the data tree yourself. Recognize: alt, altText, altTag, imageAlt*, desktopImageAlt*, mobileImageAlt*, videoAlt*, iconAlt*, featuredImageAlt, mediaAlt*. Also handle LocalizedValue objects where each locale key may be a string URL or alt text.

learnings.summary: curl -o to a path whose parent dir does not exist fails with exit code 23 and size=0 even though it prints HTTP:200. The HTTP status is returned before write, so it is misleading.
learnings.detail: On Windows bash, shell cwd can reset between tool calls, landing in a subdir. Any relative path that assumed the project root then fails. Always mkdir -p the target dir in the same bash call as the curl, or use an absolute path. Always check curl exit code, not just the HTTP status from -w.

learnings.summary: Builder.io plugin does not expose GET /assets/:id for single-asset fetch. Calling it hangs until the bash timeout. Use GET /assets?query=... or /assets?limit=... and filter by id in the response instead.
learnings.detail: The routes module registers PATCH /assets/:id and DELETE /assets/:id but no GET. An unmatched GET path falls through to a handler that never responds, so curl hits its timeout rather than getting a 404.

learnings.summary: Builder.io plugin /assets?query=<value> can time out at 30s because it paginates through the full asset library on the Builder admin API for each call. Use a larger curl --max-time (120s+) or pre-cache via /assets full scan first.
learnings.detail: Asset search is not a real server-side filter on the Builder side, the plugin scans pages and filters locally. Short timeouts will kill the request mid-scan.

learnings.summary: Before bulk-writing alt text to a Builder.io space, verify the model schema defines an alt sibling subfield. If not, the frontend component may be reading alt from a specific custom key and a PATCH that writes altText will be silently ignored by the rendered page.
learnings.detail: A Builder.io model can have a seoFields object with only metaTitle, metaDescription, featuredImage, schemaJson subfields. The Builder UI still shows a per-usage 'Alt text for this page' input, but that writes an ad hoc field the frontend may or may not render. Always map image field to alt field by inspecting the consuming component source before bulk PATCHing the Write API.

learnings.summary: Builder.io Write API enforces Firestore document limits: PATCH returns 500 with 'Input object is deeper than 20 levels or contains a cycle' when the merged data tree exceeds 20 nesting levels. Cannot patch deeply nested page entries (often Symbol-within-Symbol layouts). Workaround: edit those entries via Builder.io UI which can flatten on save, or refactor the page structure first.
learnings.detail: Hit on a Pricing page with many Columns -> Sections -> Symbols nesting. The PATCH body itself is small, but the resulting merged document violates Firestore's 20-level depth limit.

learnings.summary: When deduping CDN image URLs by their first 12 hex chars of the asset id, you can collide. Different uploads of the same source photo share a long common prefix. Always dedupe by full URL (or full asset id), not a truncated prefix.
learnings.detail: In a bulk image audit, two different Builder.io assets started with the same 12 hex chars (9b5322c33a0d) but referenced different files. The truncated-prefix dedup map silently dropped one of them and that entry got NO-OP'd.

learnings.summary: Orchestrator's claude CLI worker can hit a circuit-breaker after a few headless task failures, blocking further claude spawns until it cools down. Symptom: 'Retry failed: CLI claude circuit breaker is OPEN'. Workaround: switch to gemini, codex, or grok for that batch of work and resume claude later.
learnings.detail: Hit when spawning 4 parallel claude workers for vision tasks. Worker tasks failed initially (likely a flag/cwd issue) and the breaker tripped, locking out further claude spawns even with corrected prompts.

learnings.summary: Bash heredoc (cat <<EOF) fails with unexpected EOF when the body contains backticks or other shell metacharacters even with quoted delimiter when piped through tool boundaries. For long markdown content, use the Write tool to create the file directly, then feed it to save-note.js --file, instead of heredoc in a Bash call.
learnings.detail: Saving a ~150-line markdown note via cat > file.md << NOTEEOF ... NOTEEOF returned: line 172: unexpected EOF while looking for matching quote. Switching to Write tool for the file, then calling node scripts/save-note.js --file, worked first try.

learnings.summary: DevOps Pilot notes read endpoint is GET /api/notes/read?name={name}, not GET /api/notes/{name}. The /{name} path returns a plain text 404 Not Found and wastes a round-trip. Also URL-encode parentheses in note names: ( is %28, ) is %29 � leaving them raw can yield an empty body depending on the shell.
learnings.detail: First attempt curl /api/notes/DYOB%203.0%20Phased%20Execution%20Plan returned Not found. Correct route per /api/instructions is /api/notes/read?name=... and parens in the name must be percent-encoded.

learnings.summary: When smoke-testing an async scheduler that can spawn external processes (HTTP calls, child CLIs), keep the test to self-contained node types only (branch, approval, state mutation). Worker/tool nodes that call out to a live server will hang the test when the server is not up, and the scheduler will stay stuck in running state rather than finishing. Prefer mocking the spawn layer or covering external-facing node types with an integration test that explicitly boots the server first.
learnings.detail: Hit while writing a smoke test for a graph workflow engine. Test defined a branch + two worker nodes and expected terminal state in ~800ms. Instead, the branch correctly took one path, but the downstream worker node blocked on an HTTP call to /api/orchestrator/spawn that was never going to resolve, leaving the run status=running forever.

learnings.summary: MCP tools are ONLY for exposing DevOps Pilot capabilities to external clients (Claude Desktop, Cursor, VS Code, Zed). When building a new DevOps Pilot feature, the primary interaction surfaces are (1) a PowerShell or Node script in ./scripts/, and (2) a UI surface in the dashboard. MCP is additive icing on top of those, not a substitute. Do not ship a feature that is only reachable via MCP from outside the app.
learnings.detail: Rule from the project owner. Scripts are the canonical invocation path inside DevOps Pilot. UI gives visibility. MCP gives external interop. A complete feature ships all three, in that priority order.

learnings.summary: When persisting a workflow engine that has in-memory blocking primitives (Promise resolvers for human-approval gates, worker wait loops), the persistence layer must also persist the RESOLUTION state, not just the pending state. On restart, the in-memory resolver is gone, so clicking Approve becomes a no-op unless the engine re-enters the scheduler and recreates the waiter. Minimum viable fix: on load, reset any awaiting-approval nodes to pending and re-run the scheduler, which recreates a fresh waiter. Better fix: persist the approval decision to disk when the user clicks so a resumed run can skip past already-answered approvals without re-prompting.
learnings.detail: Hit while shipping graph-runs BETA. A run that hit an approval node and survived an app restart showed up in the approval modal, but clicking Approve returned {ok:true} silently and did nothing because approvalWaiters.get(key) returned undefined.

learnings.summary: Branch-skip propagation in a DAG scheduler must NOT blindly mark every descendant of the skipped node as skipped. A merge node that depends on both branches still has a valid path through the taken branch and should run. The correct rule: a node is only skipped when every one of its dependencies is already in the skip set; iterate to a fixed point so chained merges resolve. Separately: after marking skipped nodes as terminal (cancelled), the scheduler must continue re-checking ready nodes on the same pass, not break out of the loop, because newly-unblocked merge nodes need to be picked up immediately.
learnings.detail: Hit in the v1 graph-runs engine. Test graph: decide -> (big | small) -> summary. When small was skipped by the branch, descendantsOf(small) included summary, so summary was marked skipped too even though big was reachable. Separate bug: even after fixing the skip propagation, the scheduler broke out of its loop the moment it marked the skipped node cancelled, so the summary merge never got scheduled in the same pass.

learnings.summary: claude: API Error: Request rejected (429) · This request would exceed your organization's rate limit of 30,000 input tokens per
learnings.detail: Failed args: ["--append-system-prompt","You are a worker agent. NEVER use sleep commands. NEVER poll with curl in a loop. Just do the task and output the result. Do not dispatch sub-tasks to other CLIs. Do not call orchestrator APIs. Focus only on the task in the prompt.","--dangerously-skip-permissions","-p"]

learnings.summary: When an AI CLI worker fails with a class-of-error that strongly suggests switching models would help (token limit, rate limit, quota exceeded, context length, circuit breaker open, auth/key error, overloaded, insufficient_quota), the caller should automatically retry with a different CLI from the model router rather than surfacing the failure to the user. Track which CLIs have been tried so the router can exclude them. Cap attempts at 3 to avoid infinite loops. Non-retryable errors (bad arguments, permission deny, not-found) should fail fast without retry.
learnings.detail: Hit in graph-runs v1 where a 4-step test graph (number -> haiku -> summarize) failed on the summary node because Claude had hit its token limit. The engine surfaced the raw failure instead of transparently switching to Codex or Gemini. Fix: _runWorkerNode now loops up to 3 times, re-querying the model router with excludeClis on each retry-worthy failure.

learnings.summary: Removing a block from server.js that contains scattered unrelated helpers silently breaks consumers
learnings.detail: During a large handler extraction (Phase 3b), splice-deleting a line range in server.js accidentally removed handleImportConfig and sanitizeText, which were interleaved with the target ADO handlers but used by unrelated code paths. The dashboard lost /api/config/import and crashed on startup with "sanitizeText is not defined". Lesson: after any multi-hundred-line deletion in a monolithic server file, grep for every function name in the deleted block against the rest of the file before committing. A quick node --check passes because the calls are runtime-only. Also: watch for functions that appear to be topic-local but are actually generic utilities (sanitizeText, proxyHtmlImages patterns) -- move them to a clearly-labeled utility section before deleting the surrounding context.

learnings.summary: PowerShell curl alias prompts for Uri when given -s flag
learnings.detail: In Windows PowerShell, `curl` is an alias for Invoke-WebRequest. The -s flag prefix-matches -SessionVariable / -SkipCertificateCheck (ambiguous), causing PowerShell to discard the URL and prompt `Uri:` interactively. Use `Invoke-RestMethod <url>` (returns parsed JSON) or `curl.exe -s <url>` (real curl binary) to bypass the alias. Bash does not have this alias; curl -s works there as expected.

learnings.summary: Frontend caches plugin list once per page load; config changes mid-session can leave cache stale
learnings.detail: The dashboard initPlugins IIFE fetches /api/plugins once and caches the result in _loadedPlugins. Config-keyed plugins whose activationConditions.configKeys flip from unsatisfied to satisfied mid-session (e.g. user just saved their PAT) stay absent from the cached list until the next page load. Fix: refreshPluginActivation() diff after saveSettings() and auto-restart when the active plugin set changes, since contributions are injected once at startup and are not reversible live.

learnings.summary: Async prefix route handlers that return false silently consume requests in a naive dispatcher
learnings.detail: When a route dispatcher checks `if (result !== false) return;` against an async handler, the handler returns Promise<false>, which is truthy in that check -- so the request is treated as handled and no response is written. Browser hangs until socket timeout. Fix: if the handler returns a Promise, await it before the === false comparison. Symptom I saw: /api/plugins/registry + every /api/plugins/<x> non-config path hung forever after a generic /api/plugins/<id>/config async prefix handler was added; its fall-through path (`if (!match) return false;`) quietly swallowed all other prefix traffic.

learnings.summary: rightTabs contribution expects iframe HTML; dont use it for tabs whose rendering still lives in core
learnings.detail: Plugin SDK has TWO ways to own a right-column intel tab: (a) rightTabs = iframe-backed, plugin ships ui/<tab>.html, shell injects an iframe and a new closable button; (b) legacyNativeRightTabs = claim an existing hardcoded intelTab-<id> button, rendering logic stays in core. Mixing them up (declaring rightTabs when the rendering logic is still in core) injects duplicate buttons backed by broken iframes pointing at files the plugin does not ship. Same rule applies to centerTabs vs legacyNativeTabs. When migrating, change the manifest field AT THE SAME TIME as you move the rendering into the plugin, never before.

learnings.summary: Audit/readiness review requests are read-only unless the user explicitly asks for fixes
learnings.detail: When the user asks to compare branches, audit readiness, or check whether a branch is ready to merge, do not patch files or apply quick fixes. Report findings and recommendations only. If fixes seem obvious, ask before editing.

learnings.summary: DevOps Pilot /api/pull-request uses repoName, sourceBranch, targetBranch (not repo/head/base).
learnings.detail: POSTing {repo, head, base} to /api/pull-request returns 422 Validation Failed or "repoName is required". Correct payload: {"repoName":"<configured name>","sourceBranch":"<branch>","targetBranch":"<base>","title":"...","body":"..."}. Confirmed against the GitHub plugin route; response shape is {ok, pullRequestId, url, title}.

learnings.summary: Core shell instructions must be fully plugin-agnostic
learnings.detail: Core instruction files (INSTRUCTIONS.base.md, dashboard/instructions/*.md, /api/bootstrap payload) must not mention any specific plugin by name. That includes no sentences like -if the Azure DevOps plugin is installed- or -when the GitHub plugin is active-. Every plugin (including ADO and GitHub) must be treated identically to Builder.io, Sanity, WordPress, etc.: plugin-specific rules live inside the plugin folder at instructions.md and are fetched via /api/plugins/instructions on demand. The core should only describe the generic plugin system (discovery by keyword, ask before use, fetch that plugin instructions).

learnings.summary: PR description via /api/pull-request rendered as [object Object] when passed a string from Get-Content -Raw
learnings.detail: When creating a PR via POST /api/pull-request (or New-PullRequest.ps1), the description field did not render a multi-line string correctly and showed [object Object] in the GitHub PR body. After creating a PR, always verify the rendered description on GitHub. Safer pattern: keep description short and inline, or update the PR body via GitHub API after creation (PATCH /repos/:owner/:repo/pulls/:number with body field) if the initial render is wrong.

learnings.summary: git worktree remove fails with Filename too long when node_modules present on Windows. Mirror an empty dir over the worktree with robocopy /MIR first, then remove the now-empty dir, then git worktree prune.
learnings.detail: Deeply nested node_modules paths exceed MAX_PATH (260 chars) so git worktree remove --force errors with failed to delete <path>: Filename too long. Workaround: create an empty source dir, run robocopy.exe <empty> <worktree> /MIR /NFL /NDL /NJH /NJS /NC /NS to mirror (empties target), then Remove-Item -LiteralPath <worktree> -Recurse -Force, finally git worktree prune to clean the registry. Mirroring many node_modules is slow (tens of seconds per worktree), so run it in the background and poll the dir until it is gone.

learnings.summary: Codex workers spawned with cwd inside a Node project on Windows cannot invoke npm: the PowerShell shim fails with a StandardOutputEncoding error, so every npm command exits 1 immediately. Build verification via codex workers does not work on Windows.
learnings.detail: Root cause: PowerShell runs C:/Program Files/nodejs/npm.ps1 which executes node.exe without redirection, triggering the StandardOutputEncoding error. Workaround options: (1) run the npm commands from the supervisor own bash session with run_in_background and log to a file, then poll the log; (2) in the worker prompt, invoke npm.cmd through cmd.exe so the PowerShell wrapper is bypassed.
